headline: 467 airframes grounded across 25 states with $50M–$2B national exposure as NASAO deadline compounds with 96% Chinese propeller dependency and 97% FPV camera sourcing concentration.

lead_story.title: NASAO Compliance Deadline Grounds 467 Airframes Across 25 States
lead_story.body: Oregon NASAO Revision 2 white paper (February 2026) documents 467 drone airframes grounded or operationally restricted across 23 states based on survey of 25 state DOTs, creating $50M–$2B in national procurement exposure. The grounding reflects regulatory compliance pressure intersecting with supply chain constraints as agencies struggle to source compliant replacements. With no delta change in the past 24 hours, the situation remains static but unresolved, indicating procurement paralysis rather than resolution momentum.
lead_story.action: Conduct airframe inventory audit by May 2 to identify NASAO-affected units and submit replacement requisitions before Q3 budget lockdown; prioritize Blue UAS List vendors with confirmed delivery timelines under 90 days.

supply_chain.signal: 463 of 484 tracked propeller models (96%) manufactured in China; dominant brands HQProp, Gemfan, DAL all originate from Shenzhen/Guangdong manufacturing base
supply_chain.window: Immediate risk: propellers not considered NDAA-critical components under current interpretation, creating compliance ambiguity for programs requiring full supply chain attestation
supply_chain.action: Request legal opinion from agency counsel by May 10 on propeller classification under your specific program's Buy American or NDAA compliance requirements; identify domestic or allied-nation propeller sources as contingency suppliers

supply_chain.signal: 187 of 193 tracked FPV cameras (97%) manufactured in China; FPV cameras ARE definitively NDAA-critical components as imaging hardware with data storage/transmission capability
supply_chain.window: Critical: existing inventory may be non-compliant; replacement lead times for compliant alternatives exceed 120 days in current market
supply_chain.action: Halt new FPV camera procurement from Chinese manufacturers immediately; audit existing camera inventory for NDAA compliance and flag non-compliant units for replacement prioritization by component criticality

supply_chain.signal: 212 of 303 tracked motors (70%) are Chinese-manufactured; T-Motor/Tiger Motors dominates with 62 models from Nan'an City, Fujian, followed by iFlight and BrotherHobby
supply_chain.window: 6-9 months before current domestic motor production capacity can meet demand surge from agency transitions
supply_chain.action: Establish dual-source motor agreements with at least one non-Chinese manufacturer by June 30; build 90-day motor inventory buffer for critical airframes to bridge potential supply disruption

supply_chain.signal: GUR and Ukrainian military intelligence confirmed Raspberry Pi 4B as core component in Geran-2/Shahed-136 'Borscht Tracker V3' navigation system; 40,000+ units estimated diverted to Russian drone programs
supply_chain.window: Immediate: diversion risk creates reputational and potential sanctions exposure for agencies using RPi in non-weapons applications; export controls likely to tighten within 60 days
supply_chain.action: Document legitimate use cases and procurement chain-of-custody for all Raspberry Pi units in inventory; coordinate with agency export compliance office to ensure end-use monitoring protocols are audit-ready

gray_zone.status: Texas AG filed 7-count DTPA lawsuit February 18, 2026 in Collin County; no Anzu response filed as of April 4 (45+ days frozen); state seeks injunctive relief and civil penalties up to $10K per violation.
gray_zone.development: Independent teardowns confirm PCB layouts, board part numbers, and controller (RRC01) match DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise exactly. Firmware signed with DJI cryptographic keys with DJI retaining root key control. Product discontinuation confirmed due to adversary component shortage (DJI P1 OCU chipset embargo enforcement Q1-Q2 2026). Gray zone score 0.782 (CRITICAL).
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Federal, state, and local agencies that procured Anzu airframes under belief of non-DJI origin face compliance violations under NDAA Section 848 and potential FY2025 NDAA Section 1709 exposure; procurement justifications citing 'US-based alternative' are legally indefensible.
gray_zone.action: Ground Anzu airframes immediately pending legal review; notify agency General Counsel of potential false claims exposure; document procurement decision chain for investigative readiness; do not process pending Anzu orders.

sources.name: Texas AG Ken Paxton Lawsuit Filing

gray_zone.status: Added to FCC Covered List; existing S1 ($359) and X1 ($599) models remain available at retail but no new models can receive FCC authorization.
gray_zone.development: Analysis confirms SkyRover app connects directly to DJI cloud infrastructure with identical encryption keys (Jon Sawyer verification). OTA update pipeline touches adversary infrastructure. Shell company structure identified: SZ Knowact Robot Technology Co Ltd. Gray zone score 0.850 (CRITICAL) — highest among current gray zone entities.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Law enforcement and emergency services agencies using SkyRover S1/X1 face data exfiltration risk through DJI cloud connection; operational data including flight logs, imagery, and location data potentially accessible to adversary infrastructure.
gray_zone.action: Disable all OTA update capability on SkyRover units; implement network isolation (no internet connectivity) for mission-critical operations; transition to Blue UAS alternatives within 90 days; treat all historical flight data as potentially compromised.

watch_list.item: FCC Covered List firmware prohibition enforcement (Jan 1, 2027)
watch_list.why: Creates operational cliff for agencies still operating DJI, Autel, Anzu, SkyRover, or Cogito airframes — firmware updates prohibited, creating safety and cybersecurity exposure
watch_list.trigger: FCC release of enforcement guidance or first Notice of Apparent Liability against entity operating post-ban firmware would signal zero-tolerance enforcement posture

watch_list.item: High-energy laser C-UAS border deployment acceleration
watch_list.why: FAA-DoD safety agreement signed April 10, 2026 after New Mexico testing removes regulatory barrier; accelerates beam director and targeting component procurement for border security applications
watch_list.trigger: CBP or DoD contract award above $50M for laser C-UAS systems would validate demand signal and create component supply pressure

watch_list.why: DJI filed for reconsideration of FCC authorization revocations (Jan 2026); outcome sets precedent for Autel and other Covered List entities' legal strategies
watch_list.trigger: FCC denial of reconsideration would eliminate legal pathway for gray zone entities and accelerate market exit; approval would create regulatory uncertainty

predictions.event: Anzu Robotics product discontinuation due to adversary component shortage (DJI P1 OCU chipset embargo enforcement)
predictions.hedge: Cancel all open Anzu POs immediately; reallocate budget to Blue UAS List vendors with confirmed non-DJI supply chains (Skydio, Teal, Vantage); request vendor attestations on chipset sourcing for all new procurements

predictions.event: FCC Covered List firmware prohibition (Jan 1, 2027) creates operational cliff for agencies operating banned-entity airframes
predictions.hedge: Complete transition plan by June 30 with board-level approval; establish bridge inventory of compliant airframes by September 30 to avoid operational gap; budget 20% above current airframe costs for compliant replacements

predictions.event: High-energy laser C-UAS border deployment accelerates beam director and targeting component procurement
predictions.hedge: Engage beam director and targeting sensor suppliers now to secure allocation in anticipated CBP/DoD procurement wave; lock pricing and delivery commitments before demand surge impacts lead times

signal_summary.top_concern: component dependency concentration risk across propellers (96%), FPV cameras (97%), and motors (70%)
